name: Hana
context: gap-year student; threads: solo rail trip, learning guitar, aging family dog Bomi

episodes.text: Mapped the first leg of my rail trip: four towns down the east coast over nine days. Printed the route and pinned it above my desk with a red thread like a detective.

probes.query: How many towns and days did the first leg of my rail trip cover?
I feel excited all over again thinking of planning that route.
That chart on my wall with its red line keeps pulling my eyes toward the horizon.

episodes.text: First guitar lesson at the community center. My fingertips went numb from the strings and the teacher said that means it is working.

probes.query: What did the guitar teacher say my numb fingertips meant?
I feel a fond amusement thinking of my very first lesson.
The sting in my hands from that first practice room visit resurfaces when I begin new things.

episodes.text: Bomi could not climb the stairs tonight. She waited at the bottom step, tail low, until Dad carried her up, and I sat with her head on my knee for an hour.

probes.query: Who carried Bomi up the stairs when she couldn't climb?
I still feel a deep sadness remembering the night the dog couldn't manage the stairs.
An old companion waiting below the staircase stays lodged in me.

episodes.text: Renewed my passport photo, bought a rail pass holder, and packed a test rucksack to check the weight. Seven point three kilos, under my limit.

probes.query: How much did my test rucksack weigh?
I feel calm and ready thinking back to those trip preparations.
The quiet checklist before setting off drifts back whenever I zip a bag.

episodes.text: First night of the trip in Gangneung. I ate grilled fish alone by the harbor, terrified and thrilled in equal parts, and the sea sounded bigger in the dark.

probes.query: What did I eat by the harbor on the first night in Gangneung?
I feel that mix of thrill and fear again when the first night of the trip comes back.
Sitting by black water in a town where nobody knew my name still moves through me.

episodes.text: Missed the last bus in Samcheok and walked forty minutes to the guesthouse in drizzle. The owner left the porch light on and a note saying the kimchi fridge was fair game.

probes.query: What did the guesthouse note say was fair game?
I feel grateful remembering that soggy walk ending in kindness.
A lamp left burning for a stranger on a wet night keeps glowing somewhere in me.

episodes.text: Train home. Sorted six hundred photos into folders while the fields slid past, and ranked my favorite meals of the trip in my notebook. Fish stew won.

probes.query: Which meal won my trip food ranking?
I feel a settled contentment recalling the ride home from the trip.
Watching the countryside pass while I ordered my memories comes back on every long ride.

episodes.text: Practiced chord changes for an hour: G to C without looking, finally. Recorded a shaky clip of the campfire song and did not delete it for once.

probes.query: Which chord change did I finally manage without looking?
I feel quietly pleased about that practice breakthrough.
The first take of myself I didn't erase surfaces when doubt creeps in.

episodes.text: The vet found a mass on Bomi's spleen. Surgery is an option at her age but risky, and the three of us sat at the kitchen table not saying the obvious things.

probes.query: Where did the vet find a mass on Bomi?
The dread of that vet visit still tightens my chest.
A silent circle of family around bad news sits heavy in my memory.

episodes.text: Bomi came through the operation. The surgeon called at nine sharp, I screamed loud enough to scare the mailman, and Dad cried into the dish towel pretending to wash up.

probes.query: What time did the surgeon call about Bomi's operation?
I feel flooded with relief every time that phone call replays.
The ring that turned our house from silence into noise again keeps echoing in me.

episodes.text: Walked Bomi to the corner and back, her first outing since the stitches came out. She sniffed the same lamppost for five whole minutes and I let her.

probes.query: How long did Bomi sniff the lamppost on her first outing?
I feel soft warmth thinking of that slow little walk with her.
Standing still so an old friend could take her time returns to me often.

episodes.text: Sorted my trip laundry backlog, returned Mom's power bank, and booked a dental checkup. Also alphabetized the spice rack for no reason.

probes.query: Whose power bank did I return?
I feel neutral, mildly satisfied, about that catch-up chore day.
An afternoon of putting borrowed and scattered things back in place drifts by me.

episodes.text: Played the campfire song for my family after dinner, all the way through, only one buzzed note. Bomi thumped her tail on the floor like a metronome with opinions.

probes.query: How many buzzed notes did my family performance have?
I feel glowing happiness remembering playing that song for everyone.
Music I finally shared out loud in the living room keeps ringing in my head.

episodes.text: Watered the plants, clipped Bomi's nails, and started a packing list for the southern leg of the trip in August. Two pages already and I regret nothing.

probes.query: Which month is the southern leg of my trip planned for?
I feel calm anticipation about the list I started that evening.
A fresh page of preparations for the next departure floats into my thoughts.
